commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=f184d6d84a7bd8f82c5d8850f0e253b8d3af0ce9)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a guarded async hosted official marketplace/feed loader for the external plugin catalog, feed-shaped bundled fallback metadata, and tests for hosted fetch fallback and validation paths.

PR surface: Source +332, Tests +186, Other +5. Total +523 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the PR finding: the branch default URL is visible in source and live `curl`/`getent` checks cannot resolve `register.openclaw.ai`, while `clawhub.ai` resolves and serves a feed. Current main has no hosted loader, so this is branch-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Manual review is needed because the PR is draft/protected, the endpoint choice is product-sensitive, and the missing live proof cannot be supplied by an automated repair worker.

target.title: Add hosted external catalog feed loader
target.body:
<!-- hosted-feeds-stack-context:start -->
## Hosted feeds stack context

This PR is PR2 in the hosted feeds implementation stack. The stack implements the model proposed in [RFC 19](https://github.com/openclaw/rfcs/pull/19).

Review order:
- [RFC 19](https://github.com/openclaw/rfcs/pull/19): hosted feeds for plugins and skills
- [#95846](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/95846) PR1: feed-shaped bundled fallback
- [#95868](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/95868) PR2: guarded hosted fetch
- [#95877](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/95877) PR3: last-known-good snapshot fallback
- [#95964](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/95964) PR4: persistent SQLite snapshots
- [#95969](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/95969) PR5: local source profile validation
- [#95981](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/95981) PR6: OpenClaw config profiles

This PR's role: Adds the lazy hosted marketplace/feed fetch path with HTTPS/SSRF guardrails and bundled fallback, without making hosted fetch part of startup yet.

Stack boundaries: the current stack is marketplace/feed plumbing only. It does not auto-install packages, does not switch installer registries, does not execute feed/source auth, and does not enforce signed feed envelopes yet. Those are later slices after Vince and the maintainers are happy with the core fallback and profile model.
<!-- hosted-feeds-stack-context:end -->

## Journey Context

This is step 2 of the hosted OpenClaw feed path from RFC 19. The stack is [#95846](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/95846) -> [#95868](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/95868) -> [#95877](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/95877). #95846 made the bundled catalog feed-shaped. This PR adds the guarded hosted fetch path for that same marketplace feed, while leaving the existing synchronous bundled-catalog startup path unchanged.

## What This PR Does

- Adds an async hosted loader for `https://register.openclaw.ai/official-marketplace.json`.
- Routes hosted fetches through the existing SSRF guard with HTTPS required and the register host allowlisted.
- Sends optional `If-None-Match` and `If-Modified-Since` headers so callers can perform freshness checks.
- Reads response bodies with byte and chunk-timeout limits.
- Keeps the network guard import lazy so bundled-catalog cold paths do not load the guarded network stack.
- Computes `sha256` metadata for the hosted response body.
- Validates the hosted document against the feed-shaped official external plugin catalog schema.
- Falls back to the bundled catalog on network, status, checksum, size, JSON, or schema failures.

## What This PR Intentionally Does Not Do

This PR does not wire the hosted loader into startup, add persistent snapshots, add source-profile selection, add auth, add signed envelopes, add regional feeds, or change plugin install/search behavior. #95877 adds the loader-level last-known-good snapshot contract on top of this fetch path.

## How To Review

Review this as the hosted transport and validation layer. The important contract is: hosted marketplace feed can be tried safely, but any unsafe or unusable hosted result falls back to the bundled catalog instead of changing current behavior.

## Validation

- `pnpm exec oxfmt src/plugins/official-external-plugin-catalog.ts src/plugins/official-external-plugin-catalog.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxlint --deny-warnings src/plugins/official-external-plugin-catalog.ts src/plugins/official-external-plugin-catalog.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `CI=1 timeout 180s node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/plugins/official-external-plugin-catalog.test.ts` (22 tests)
- `timeout 300s pnpm tsgo:core:test`
